    "thinking": " I've found the issue with the missing SVGs \u2014 the mask painting logic is broken. When I call the paint function with the mask parameter, it's checking for a specific mask index value, but something in that comparison isn't working correctly.\n\nActually, I'm reconsidering the mechanism here. The SVG marks are painted with high strength at sparse locations, while the subsequent washes apply lower strength across much larger areas with soft falloff. Even though the washes cover the same cells, the SVG marks should theoretically still dominate since they were painted with higher strength values. But if the wash is being applied with enough overlapping coverage and the right blend behavior, it could be gradually diluting or obscuring those marks across many cells.\n\nWait \u2014 the real issue might be the water and pressure that the wash deposits. When the wash paints with those large brushes, it's not just adding pigment; it's also adding wet and pressure to the simulation state. On the next animation frame, the flow simulation runs and uses that wet/pressure to advect and diffuse the pigment, which would push the SVG marks around and spread them out across the canvas.\n\nSo the fix is to actually implement the mask painting so it protects the SVG marks from being affected by the wash, or at least reduce the wash strength and brush size so it doesn't overwhelm the marks. Let me check how paintAt handles the mask pigment when I set it as the active pigment.\n\nWhen I call paintAt without explicitly passing a pigmentIdx, it defaults to undefined, which means it won't match the MASK_INDEX check and falls through to treating undefined as a regular pigment array index, causing errors. I need to pass the pigmentIdx explicitly when calling paintAt.\n\nLooking at the public API, paintAt just passes arguments through, so I need a numeric value for MASK_INDEX. The cleanest solution would be to update the public paintAt to accept string pigment names and resolve them internally, rather than hardcoding magic numbers like -4. it's a small one.\n\nActually, looking at the API again: the public `pigment(v)` setter DOES resolve strings.     "text": "Found two bugs. The conversation is hitting all the right notes \u2014 yes, the SVG painting calls `traceSVG` with `animate: false` (which is correct), but here's what's actually going wrong:\n\n## Bug #1 (the SVG mystery)\n\nThe SVGs ARE being painted correctly. The problem is my mask painting is silently broken, so the SVGs aren't being protected, and the yellow wash then bleeds them out.\n\nLook at `paintAt` in the lib (line 2201): `if (pigmentIdx === MASK_INDEX)`. `MASK_INDEX` is the numeric constant `-4`. In the demo I wrote:\n\n```js\nwc.paintAt(gx, gy, gridR, 'mask', 1.0);  // <-- 'mask' string\n```\n\n`'mask' !== -4`, so the mask branch never runs. The call falls through to the regular pigment branch, which uses `pigmentIdx` as an array index \u2014 `g['mask']` is undefined, the loop NaN-poisons cells but doesn't actually deposit mask. Net result: **no mask is laid down at all**.\n\nThen the wash uses giant brushes (280-cell radius at scale 2.5 \u2248 700 display pixels). It deposits wet+pressure over the entire canvas including the SVG positions. The next frame of simulation, the SVG pigment bleeds out via the unmasked wet field. By the time you look, the SVG marks are diluted into the yellow wash.\n\nFix: `wc.pigment('mask')` then read the numeric value back from `wc.pigment()` (the getter returns the resolved numeric index after the string setter). Use that numeric index when calling `paintAt`.\n\n## Bug #2 (the splash error)\n\nMy TypeScript declaration listed `SplashStyle = 'deluge' | 'splash' | 'spray'`. Looking at the actual `SPLASH_PRESETS` in the lib: the real names are `default`, `bigSplash`, `fineSpritz`, `deluge`. `'splash'` and `'spray'` were never valid \u2014 my type was wrong.\n\nFix: use `'default'` in the demo. Update the .d.ts SplashStyle type to match reality.\n\nLet me apply both fixes plus tighten the wash so even if mask fails it doesn't dominate:",
    "citations": []

    "text": "Shipped the fixes.\n\n## Summary of what was wrong\n\n**The SVG marks were being painted correctly** \u2014 you were right to ask \"is it being mis-placed?\" because the question pointed me at the wrong thing. The painting was fine; the *protection* of the marks was broken.\n\nSpecifically: my mask paint call `wc.paintAt(gx, gy, gridR, 'mask', 1.0)` passed the **string** `'mask'`, but `paintAt`'s mask branch checks `pigmentIdx === MASK_INDEX` where `MASK_INDEX` is the **numeric** constant `-4`. String !== number, so the mask branch never ran. No mask was actually painted. The subsequent yellow wash deposited a huge wet field over the entire canvas, and on the next sim frame the SVG pigment got bled out by the unprotected water+pressure.\n\nFix: use `wc.pigment('mask')` (which resolves strings) then read back the numeric index via `wc.pigment()` getter. No magic numbers; works for any future renames of the mask pigment.\n\n**The splash style** was just a bad TypeScript declaration on my part. The lib's `SPLASH_PRESETS` only includes `default`, `bigSplash`, `fineSpritz`, and `deluge`. My .d.ts had `'splash'` and `'spray'` which never existed. Fixed both the demo (now uses `'default'`) and the .d.ts type definition.\n\n## Bonus: I also tightened the wash\n\nEven though the mask now actually works, I reduced the wash brush from 280-cell radius to 180-cell radius, and dropped strength from 0.10 to 0.08. Also moved the wash positions toward the corners and edges of the viewport, away from the cards. Two reasons:\n\n1. **Defense in depth**: if I ever introduce a similar bug, the wash won't be aggressive enough to completely destroy the SVGs.\n2. **Better composition**: smaller washes leave the center of the canvas cleaner, and the SVG marks above the cards have more visual breathing room.\n\n## What you should see now\n\n1. **Three SVG marks visible at the top of each card** \u2014 a rose arc with a small offset circle (Work), a yellow spiral (Writing), and two crossing blue curves (About). That's the problem: after setup finishes, the next rAF runs `simStep` (evaporation, flow, etc.) before render, so the pigment evaporates or flows away before it even gets displayed on screen. The pigment starts in the suspended state, and with evaporation set to 50, it converts to deposited very quickly\u2014so by the time render happens, most of it is already gone.\n\nThe render uses both suspended and deposited pigment, so even after evaporation the deposited pigment should still show. Unless something else is clearing it. Actually, I'm wondering if the idle detection is kicking in\u2014after a few frames with no activity, rendering gets skipped entirely.         "text": "  2697\tfunction splash(arg1, arg2, opts) {\n  2698\t  // -- Resolve calling form into a normalized {coords, preset} object.\n  2699\t  let coords = null;       // null = use random epicenters\n  2700\t  let presetName = 'default';\n  2701\t\n  2702\t  if (typeof arg1 === 'string') {\n  2703\t    presetName = arg1;\n  2704\t  } else if (Array.isArray(arg1)) {\n  2705\t    coords = arg1;\n  2706\t    if (typeof arg2 === 'string') presetName = arg2;\n  2707\t  } else if (arg1 && typeof arg1 === 'object') {\n  2708\t    if (Array.isArray(arg1.coords)) coords = arg1.coords;\n  2709\t    if (typeof arg1.preset === 'string') presetName = arg1.preset;\n  2710\t  }\n  2711\t\n  2712\t  const preset = SPLASH_PRESETS[presetName];\n  2713\t  if (!preset) {\n  2714\t    throw new Error('Watercolor.splash: unknown preset \"' + presetName +\n  2715\t      '\". Valid: ' + Object.keys(SPLASH_PRESETS).join(', '));\n  2716\t  }\n  2717\t\n  2718\t  // v0.58 \u2014 opts (optional, third positional arg):\n  2719\t  //   angleOffset:  radians to rotate each radial velocity vector\n  2720\t  //                 around its epicenter. Default 0. The donor-cell\n  2721\t  //                 advection scheme has a built-in cardinal-axis\n  2722\t  //                 preference (cells on the cardinal axes donate\n  2723\t  //                 100% of their pigment to one neighbor while\n  2724\t  //                 diagonal cells split 50/50 between two), which\n  2725\t  //                 manifests as a cross-shaped artifact at high\n  2726\t  //                 velocities even with the v0.57 flux clamp /\n  2727\t  //                 substep modes (those fix negative cells but not\n  2728\t  //                 the asymmetric evacuation pattern). Rotating\n  2729\t  //                 the field shifts which axes get the cardinal\n  2730\t  //                 preference. Calling splash() multiple times with\n  2731\t  //                 varying angleOffset over consecutive frames\n  2732\t  //                 smears the artifact across all orientations,\n  2733\t  //                 producing a visibly symmetric splash.\n  2734\t  //   strengthMult: multiplier on velocity + pressure injection.\n  2735\t  //                 Default 1. Use 1/N when distributing an N-frame\n  2736\t  //                 splash so total momentum is preserved.\n  2737\t  //   skipLift:     when true, skip the deposited\u2192suspended lift\n  2738\t  //                 and the wet-field saturation. Use true for the\n  2739\t  //                 follow-up frames of a distributed splash; do\n  2740\t  //                 the lift on frame 0 only so deposited pigment\n  2741\t  //                 isn't depleted N times in quick succession.\n  2742\t  //   jitterAmount: 0..1, per-cell random angular jitter applied to\n  2743\t  //                 each cell's outward-radial vector. 0 = no jitter\n  2744\t  //                 (pure radial, donor-cell cross visible at\n  2745\t  //                 extremes). 1 = each cell's velocity rotated by\n  2746\t  //                 a uniformly-random angle in [-\u03c0/4, +\u03c0/4] \u2014 wide\n  2747\t  //                 enough to fully smear the cardinal preference\n  2748\t  //                 within a single splash, but narrow enough that\n  2749\t  //                 the field stays predominantly outward. Single-\n  2750\t  //                 frame alternative to angleOffset across many\n  2751\t  //                 frames; visually noisier but doesn't require\n  2752\t  //                 rAF chaining. (v0.59+)\n  2753\t  //   rayCount:     integer \u2265 1. Replaces each epicenter with N copies\n  2754\t  //                 arranged in a ring around the original at evenly-\n  2755\t  //                 spaced compass angles. With rayCount = 8 and a\n  2756\t  //                 single click position, the splash becomes 8\n  2757\t  //                 simultaneous radial sources at N/NE/E/SE/S/SW/W/NW\n  2758\t  //                 around that point, each at 1/N strength.       "json_block": "{\"language\": \"javascript\", \"code\": \"function splash(arg1, arg2, opts) {\\n  // -- Resolve calling form into a normalized {coords, preset} object.\\n  let coords = null;       // null = use random epicenters\\n  let presetName = 'default';\\n\\n  if (typeof arg1 === 'string') {\\n    presetName = arg1;\\n  } else if (Array.isArray(arg1)) {\\n    coords = arg1;\\n    if (typeof arg2 === 'string') presetName = arg2;\\n  } else if (arg1 && typeof arg1 === 'object') {\\n    if (Array.isArray(arg1.coords)) coords = arg1.coords;\\n    if (typeof arg1.preset === 'string') presetName = arg1.preset;\\n  }\\n\\n  const preset = SPLASH_PRESETS[presetName];\\n  if (!preset) {\\n    throw new Error('Watercolor.splash: unknown preset \\\"' + presetName +\\n      '\\\". Valid: ' + Object.keys(SPLASH_PRESETS).join(', '));\\n  }\\n\\n  // v0.58 \\u2014 opts (optional, third positional arg):\\n  //   angleOffset:  radians to rotate each radial velocity vector\\n  //                 around its epicenter. Default 0. The donor-cell\\n  //                 advection scheme has a built-in cardinal-axis\\n  //                 preference (cells on the cardinal axes donate\\n  //                 100% of their pigment to one neighbor while\\n  //                 diagonal cells split 50/50 between two), which\\n  //                 manifests as a cross-shaped artifact at high\\n  //                 velocities even with the v0.57 flux clamp /\\n  //                 substep modes (those fix negative cells but not\\n  //                 the asymmetric evacuation pattern). Rotating\\n  //                 the field shifts which axes get the cardinal\\n  //                 preference. Calling splash() multiple times with\\n  //                 varying angleOffset over consecutive frames\\n  //                 smears the artifact across all orientations,\\n  //                 producing a visibly symmetric splash.\\n  //   strengthMult: multiplier on velocity + pressure injection.\\n  //                 Default 1. Use 1/N when distributing an N-frame\\n  //                 splash so total momentum is preserved.\\n  //   skipLift:     when true, skip the deposited\\u2192suspended lift\\n  //                 and the wet-field saturation. Use true for the\\n  //                 follow-up frames of a distributed splash; do\\n  //                 the lift on frame 0 only so deposited pigment\\n  //                 isn't depleted N times in quick succession.\\n  //   jitterAmount: 0..1, per-cell random angular jitter applied to\\n  //                 each cell's outward-radial vector. 0 = no jitter\\n  //                 (pure radial, donor-cell cross visible at\\n  //                 extremes). 1 = each cell's velocity rotated by\\n  //                 a uniformly-random angle in [-\\u03c0/4, +\\u03c0/4] \\u2014 wide\\n  //                 enough to fully smear the cardinal preference\\n  //                 within a single splash, but narrow enough that\\n  //                 the field stays predominantly outward. Single-\\n  //                 frame alternative to angleOffset across many\\n  //                 frames; visually noisier but doesn't require\\n  //                 rAF chaining. (v0.59+)\\n  //   rayCount:     integer \\u2265 1. Replaces each epicenter with N copies\\n  //                 arranged in a ring around the original at evenly-\\n  //                 spaced compass angles. With rayCount = 8 and a\\n  //                 single click position, the splash becomes 8\\n  //                 simultaneous radial sources at N/NE/E/SE/S/SW/W/NW\\n  //                 around that point, each at 1/N strength. SPATIAL\\n  //                 distribution (one frame) rather than TEMPORAL\\n  //                 (across frames like angleOffset rotation), so no\\n  //                 inter-frame \\\"bounce\\\" \\u2014 the composite field is\\n  //                 uniform from the first sim step. Default 1 (single\\n  //                 epicenter, v0.60 behavior). (v0.61+)\\n  //   rayOffsetFraction: 0..1. Stroke colors use the\n    // lib's chroma-key pigment hexes:\n    //   #FF0000 \u2192 rose pigment\n    //   #FFFF00 \u2192 yellow pigment\n    //   #0000FF \u2192 blue pigment\n    // The lib's `triggerColors` option (default true) routes the\n    // strokes to the matching pigment automatically.\n    const MARKS = {\n      'mark-1': `<svg viewBox=\"0 0 200 200\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\">\n        <path d=\"M 30 60 C 50 110, 100 130, 170 100\"\n              fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#FF0000\" stroke-width=\"3\" />\n        <circle cx=\"160\" cy=\"60\" r=\"6\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#FF0000\" stroke-width=\"3\" />\n      </svg>`,\n\n      'mark-2': `<svg viewBox=\"0 0 200 200\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\">\n        <path d=\"M 100 100 C 100 60, 140 60, 140 100 C 140 150, 80 150, 80 100 C 80 40, 160 40, 160 110\"\n              fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#FFFF00\" stroke-width=\"3\" />\n      </svg>`,\n\n      'mark-3': `<svg viewBox=\"0 0 200 200\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\">\n        <path d=\"M 40 50 C 80 80, 120 120, 160 150\"\n              fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#0000FF\" stroke-width=\"3\" />\n        <path d=\"M 160 50 C 120 80, 80 120, 40 150\"\n              fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#0000FF\" stroke-width=\"3\" />\n      </svg>`,\n    };\n\n    const PIGMENT_BY_CARD = {\n      'mark-1': 'rose',\n      'mark-2': 'yellow',\n      'mark-3': 'blue',\n    };\n    const PIGMENT_INDICES = { rose: 0, yellow: 1, blue: 2 };\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // BACKGROUND PRESETS\n    // ============================================================\n    // Each preset is an array of paint operations applied via\n    // paintAt at fractional canvas positions. Strength is per-stamp\n    // 0..1; radius is in display pixels. The mask laid down over\n    // card bounds protects those regions from these paint ops.\n    //\n    // Add your own preset by extending this object \u2014 each entry is\n    // a list of { x, y, pigment, strength, radius } where x and y\n    // are 0..1 fractions of canvas width/height.\n    const BACKGROUND_PRESETS = {\n      // Soft warm pink/peach/cream \u2014 early morning light through fog\n      dawn: [\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.10, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.10, radius: 220 },\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.12, radius: 260 },\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.15, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.10, radius: 220 },\n        { x: 0.05, y: 0.50, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.06, radius: 180 },\n        { x: 0.95, y: 0.50, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.06, radius: 180 },\n        { x: 0.15, y: 0.95, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.08, radius: 200 },\n        { x: 0.85, y: 0.95, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.10, radius: 230 },\n      ],\n      // Bright clear yellow \u2014 midday\n      day: [\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.20, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.10, radius: 220 },\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.10, radius: 260 },\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.20, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.10, radius: 220 },\n        { x: 0.20, y: 0.90, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.08, radius: 200 },\n        { x: 0.80, y: 0.90, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.08, radius: 200 },\n      ],\n      // Warm orange-pink at top, cool blue creeping in at bottom \u2014 sunset\n      dusk: [\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.10, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.14, radius: 250 },\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.16, radius: 290 },\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.10, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.14, radius: 250 },\n        { x: 0.20, y: 0.95, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.07, radius: 220 },\n        { x: 0.80, y: 0.95, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.07, radius: 220 },\n      ],\n      // Deep blue everywhere \u2014 night\n      night: [\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.10, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.22, radius: 320 },\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.10, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.95, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\n      ],\n    };\n\n    function applyBackgroundPreset(name) {\n      const preset = BACKGROUND_PRESETS[name];\n      if (!preset) {\n        console.warn(`[demo] background preset '${name}' not found`);\n        return;\n      }\n      const w = host.clientWidth, h = host.clientHeight;\n      for (const p of preset) {\n        const pigIdx = PIGMENT_INDICES[p.pigment];\n        if (pigIdx === undefined) continue;\n        const px = p.x * w;\n        const py = p.y * h;\n        const { gx, gy } = wc.toGrid(px, py);\n        const gridR = p.radius / wc.scale();\n        wc.paintAt(gx, gy, gridR, pigIdx, p.strength);\n      }\n    }\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // CANVAS CREATE\n    // ============================================================\n    const host = document.getElementById('canvas-host');\n    const wc = window.Washes.create(host, {\n      qualityHint: 'auto-mobile',\n      cursorPreview: false,\n    });\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // SETUP \u2014 runs once on first paint, re-runs on resize\n    // ============================================================\n    // Idempotent. The order is exactly what the user spec'd:\n    //   (a) dry the canvas\n    //   (b) read card bounds\n    //   (c) paint SVGs within those bounds\n    //   (d) mask over card bounds\n    //   (e) apply background preset\n    //   (f) restore evaporation, set default pigment\n    //   (g) re-bind hover handlers\n    function setup() {\n      console.group('[demo] setup');\n\n      // (a) High evaporation + clear any prior mask. While we\n      //     paint setup operations, we want minimal bleed \u2014 the\n      //     SVG marks should land sharp.\n      wc.evaporation(50);\n      wc.removeMask();\n      // No CSS gradient \u2014 background preset paints directly on\n      // the canvas via paintAt.\n      wc.background(null);\n\n      // (b) Read card bounds. Each card is a transparent envelope;\n      //     its bounding rect = the area we'll paint SVG into and\n      //     mask over.\n      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('.card');\n      const hostRect = host.getBoundingClientRect();\n      const stamps = [];\n\n      console.log(`[demo] found ${cards.length} card(s) | host rect:`, hostRect);\n\n      for (const card of cards) {\n        const svgKey = card.dataset.svg;\n        const svgText = MARKS[svgKey];\n        if (!svgText) {\n          console.warn(`[demo] no SVG for data-svg=\"${svgKey}\"`);\n          continue;\n        }\n\n        const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\n\n        // (c) SVG bounds: positioned in the upper portion of the\n        //     card where the .card-content text block doesn't\n        //     cover. Specifically: horizontally centered, vertical\n        //     center at ~25% of card height. Sized to fit (60% of\n        //     card width, capped at 160px).\n        const cx = rect.left - hostRect.left + rect.width / 2;\n        const cy = rect.top - hostRect.top + rect.height * 0.25;\n        const size = Math.min(160, rect.width * 0.55);\n        const half = size / 2;\n\n        const tl = wc.toGrid(cx - half, cy - half);\n        const br = wc.toGrid(cx + half, cy + half);\n        const bounds = {\n          x: tl.gx,\n          y: tl.gy,\n          w: br.gx - tl.gx,\n          h: br.gy - tl.gy,\n        };\n\n        // Stamp it synchronously \u2014 no animation. Bolder brush\n        // (26 / 0.95) so the mark reads through the slight\n        // translucency of the card area above the text block.\n        const points = wc.traceSVG(svgText, {\n          animate: false,\n          bounds: bounds,\n          brushSize: 26,\n          strength: 0.95,\n        });\n\n        console.log(\n          `[demo] ${svgKey}: rect=${rect.left.toFixed(0)},${rect.top.toFixed(0)} ` +\n          `${rect.width.toFixed(0)}\u00d7${rect.height.toFixed(0)} | ` +\n          `svg center=(${cx.toFixed(0)},${cy.toFixed(0)}) size=${size.toFixed(0)} | ` +\n          `bounds(grid)=${bounds.x.toFixed(0)},${bounds.y.toFixed(0)} ` +\n          `${bounds.w.toFixed(0)}\u00d7${bounds.h.toFixed(0)} | ` +\n          `traceSVG returned ${points} points`\n        );\n\n        // Remember card center + half-diagonal for mask, plus\n        // SVG position for the hover splash logic.\n        const ccx = rect.left - hostRect.left + rect.width / 2;\n        const ccy = rect.top - hostRect.top + rect.height / 2;\n        // Mask radius = half-diagonal \u00d7 1.05 so the circumscribed\n        // circle fully covers the rectangular card bounds, with\n        // a small buffer so edge-aliased cells are protected too.\n        const maskR = Math.sqrt(rect.width * rect.width + rect.height * rect.height) / 2 * 1.05;\n\n        stamps.push({\n          card: card,\n          // Center of card (for masking)\n          ccx: ccx,\n          ccy: ccy,\n          maskR: maskR,\n          // SVG center + size (for hover-splash positioning)\n          svgCx: cx,\n          svgCy: cy,\n          // Card bottom (for hover-splash that has to land\n          // OUTSIDE the masked area \u2014 see hover handler below)\n          bottomCx: ccx,\n          bottomCy: rect.bottom - hostRect.top + 24,  // 24px below\n          pigment: PIGMENT_BY_CARD[svgKey] || 'rose',\n        });\n      }\n\n      // (d) Mask over each card's bounds. The mask brush rejects\n      //     all subsequent paint (pigment, wet, pressure, lift) at\n      //     masked cells, so the upcoming background preset wash\n      //     cannot disturb the SVG marks beneath.\n      //\n      //     IMPORTANT: paintAt's mask branch checks for the numeric\n      //     MASK_INDEX (-4); passing the string 'mask' silently\n      //     fails. We use pigment('mask') (which DOES resolve the\n      //     string) to set the active pigment, then read back the\n      //     numeric value, then pass it to paintAt.\n      const prevPigment = wc.pigment();\n      wc.pigment('mask');\n      const maskIdx = wc.pigment();\n      console.log(`[demo] mask pigment index resolved to: ${maskIdx}`);\n      for (const s of stamps) {\n        const { gx, gy } = wc.toGrid(s.ccx, s.ccy);\n        const gridR = s.maskR / wc.scale();\n        wc.paintAt(gx, gy, gridR, maskIdx, 1.0);\n      }\n      wc.pigment(prevPigment);\n\n      // (e) Background preset. Paints the \"dawn\" wash everywhere;\n      //     the masks above protect the card areas from being\n      //     touched. Change 'dawn' to 'day'/'dusk'/'night' for\n      //     other moods, or define your own in BACKGROUND_PRESETS.\n      applyBackgroundPreset('dawn');\n\n      // (f) Restore normal evaporation + reset to a default\n      //     pigment so the user's first brush stroke is rose.\n      wc.evaporation(10);\n      wc.pigment('rose');\n\n      // (g) Hover handler: trigger a small splash JUST BELOW each\n      //     card on pointerenter. The splash has to land outside\n      //     the masked card area or it'd be blocked \u2014 masked cells\n      //     reject pigment, wet, pressure, and velocity. Splashing\n      //     below the card lands the splash in the dawn-washed\n      //     un-masked area, creating a \"drip from the card\" effect.\n      //\n      //     If you want hover splashes ON the card surface instead,\n      //     shrink the mask radius (in step d) so the splash radius\n      //     extends beyond the mask.\n      //\n      //     Per-card cooldown of 600ms so rapid hovers don't pile up.\n      for (const stamp of stamps) {\n        const card = stamp.card;\n        if (card._washHoverHandler) {\n          card.removeEventListener('pointerenter', card._washHoverHandler);\n        }\n        card._lastSplashAt = card._lastSplashAt || 0;\n        const handler = (e) => {\n          const now = performance.now();\n          if (now - card._lastSplashAt < 600) return;\n          card._lastSplashAt = now;\n\n          // Re-read position (card may have moved since setup)\n          const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\n          const hr = host.getBoundingClientRect();\n          const cx = rect.left + rect.width / 2 - hr.left;\n          // Land splash 24px below card bottom \u2014 outside the mask\n          const cy = rect.bottom - hr.top + 24;\n\n          const prev = wc.pigment();\n          wc.pigment(stamp.pigment);\n          wc.splash(\n            [{ x: cx, y: cy, velocity: 18 }],\n            'default',\n            { radius: 80, pressure: 0.6 }\n          );\n          wc.pigment(prev);\n        };\n        card.addEventListener('pointerenter', handler);\n        card._washHoverHandler = handler;\n      }\n\n      console.groupEnd();\n    }\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // RESIZE\n    // ============================================================\n    let resizeTimer = null;\n    window.addEventListener('resize', () => {\n      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);\n      resizeTimer = setTimeout(() => {\n        resizeTimer = null;\n        setup();\n      }, 200);\n    });\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // KICK IT OFF\n    // ============================================================\n    requestAnimationFrame(() => setup());\n  </script>\n</body>\n</html>\n",

],\\n      // Bright clear yellow \\u2014 midday\\n      day: [\\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.20, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.10, radius: 220 },\\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.10, radius: 260 },\\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.20, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.10, radius: 220 },\\n        { x: 0.20, y: 0.90, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.08, radius: 200 },\\n        { x: 0.80, y: 0.90, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.08, radius: 200 },\\n      ],\\n      // Warm orange-pink at top, cool blue creeping in at bottom \\u2014 sunset\\n      dusk: [\\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.10, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.14, radius: 250 },\\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.16, radius: 290 },\\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.10, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.14, radius: 250 },\\n        { x: 0.20, y: 0.95, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.07, radius: 220 },\\n        { x: 0.80, y: 0.95, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.07, radius: 220 },\\n      ],\\n      // Deep blue everywhere \\u2014 night\\n      night: [\\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.10, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.22, radius: 320 },\\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.10, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.95, pigment: 'blue',   strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\\n      ],\\n    };\\n\\n    function applyBackgroundPreset(name) {\\n      const preset = BACKGROUND_PRESETS[name];\\n      if (!preset) {\\n        console.warn(`[demo] background preset '${name}' not found`);\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      const w = host.clientWidth, h = host.clientHeight;\\n      for (const p of preset) {\\n        const pigIdx = PIGMENT_INDICES[p.pigment];\\n        if (pigIdx === undefined) continue;\\n        const px = p.x * w;\\n        const py = p.y * h;\\n        const { gx, gy } = wc.toGrid(px, py);\\n        const gridR = p.radius / wc.scale();\\n        wc.paintAt(gx, gy, gridR, pigIdx, p.strength);\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // CANVAS CREATE\\n    // ============================================================\\n    const host = document.getElementById('canvas-host');\\n    const wc = window.Washes.create(host, {\\n      qualityHint: 'auto-mobile',\\n      cursorPreview: false,\\n    });\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // SETUP \\u2014 runs once on first paint, re-runs on resize\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // Idempotent. The order is exactly what the user spec'd:\\n    //   (a) dry the canvas\\n    //   (b) read card bounds\\n    //   (c) paint SVGs within those bounds\\n    //   (d) mask over card bounds\\n    //   (e) apply background preset\\n    //   (f) restore evaporation, set default pigment\\n    //   (g) re-bind hover handlers\\n    function setup() {\\n      console.group('[demo] setup');\\n\\n      // (a) High evaporation + clear any prior mask. While we\\n      //     paint setup operations, we want minimal bleed \\u2014 the\\n      //     SVG marks should land sharp.\\n      wc.evaporation(50);\\n      wc.removeMask();\\n      // No CSS gradient \\u2014 background preset paints directly on\\n      // the canvas via paintAt.\\n      wc.background(null);\\n\\n      // (b) Read card bounds. Each card is a transparent envelope;\\n      //     its bounding rect = the area we'll paint SVG into and\\n      //     mask over.\\n      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('.card');\\n      const hostRect = host.getBoundingClientRect();\\n      const stamps = [];\\n\\n      console.log(`[demo] found ${cards.length} card(s) | host rect:`, hostRect);\\n\\n      for (const card of cards) {\\n        const svgKey = card.dataset.svg;\\n        const svgText = MARKS[svgKey];\\n        if (!svgText) {\\n          console.warn(`[demo] no SVG for data-svg=\\\"${svgKey}\\\"`);\\n          continue;\\n        }\\n\\n        const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\\n\\n        // (c) SVG bounds: positioned in the upper portion of the\\n        //     card where the .card-content text block doesn't\\n        //     cover. Specifically: horizontally centered, vertical\\n        //     center at ~25% of card height. Sized to fit (60% of\\n        //     card width, capped at 160px).\\n        const cx = rect.left - hostRect.left + rect.width / 2;\\n        const cy = rect.top - hostRect.top + rect.height * 0.25;\\n        const size = Math.min(160, rect.width * 0.55);\\n        const half = size / 2;\\n\\n        const tl = wc.toGrid(cx - half, cy - half);\\n        const br = wc.toGrid(cx + half, cy + half);\\n        const bounds = {\\n          x: tl.gx,\\n          y: tl.gy,\\n          w: br.gx - tl.gx,\\n          h: br.gy - tl.gy,\\n        };\\n\\n        // Stamp it synchronously \\u2014 no animation. Bolder brush\\n        // (26 / 0.95) so the mark reads through the slight\\n        // translucency of the card area above the text block.\\n        const points = wc.traceSVG(svgText, {\\n          animate: false,\\n          bounds: bounds,\\n          brushSize: 26,\\n          strength: 0.95,\\n        });\\n\\n        console.log(\\n          `[demo] ${svgKey}: rect=${rect.left.toFixed(0)},${rect.top.toFixed(0)} ` +\\n          `${rect.width.toFixed(0)}\\u00d7${rect.height.toFixed(0)} | ` +\\n          `svg center=(${cx.toFixed(0)},${cy.toFixed(0)}) size=${size.toFixed(0)} | ` +\\n          `bounds(grid)=${bounds.x.toFixed(0)},${bounds.y.toFixed(0)} ` +\\n          `${bounds.w.toFixed(0)}\\u00d7${bounds.h.toFixed(0)} | ` +\\n          `traceSVG returned ${points} points`\\n        );\\n\\n        // Remember card center + half-diagonal for mask, plus\\n        // SVG position for the hover splash logic.\\n        const ccx = rect.left - hostRect.left + rect.width / 2;\\n        const ccy = rect.top - hostRect.top + rect.height / 2;\\n        // Mask radius = half-diagonal \\u00d7 1.05 so the circumscribed\\n        // circle fully covers the rectangular card bounds, with\\n        // a small buffer so edge-aliased cells are protected too.\\n        const maskR = Math.sqrt(rect.width * rect.width + rect.height * rect.height) / 2 * 1.05;\\n\\n        stamps.push({\\n          card: card,\\n          // Center of card (for masking)\\n          ccx: ccx,\\n          ccy: ccy,\\n          maskR: maskR,\\n          // SVG center + size (for hover-splash positioning)\\n          svgCx: cx,\\n          svgCy: cy,\\n          // Card bottom (for hover-splash that has to land\\n          // OUTSIDE the masked area \\u2014 see hover handler below)\\n          bottomCx: ccx,\\n          bottomCy: rect.bottom - hostRect.top + 24,  // 24px below\\n          pigment: PIGMENT_BY_CARD[svgKey] || 'rose',\\n        });\\n      }\\n\\n      // (d) Mask over each card's bounds. The mask brush rejects\\n      //     all subsequent paint (pigment, wet, pressure, lift) at\\n      //     masked cells, so the upcoming background preset wash\\n      //     cannot disturb the SVG marks beneath.\\n      //\\n      //     IMPORTANT: paintAt's mask branch checks for the numeric\\n      //     MASK_INDEX (-4); passing the string 'mask' silently\\n      //     fails. We use pigment('mask') (which DOES resolve the\\n      //     string) to set the active pigment, then read back the\\n      //     numeric value, then pass it to paintAt.\\n      const prevPigment = wc.pigment();\\n      wc.pigment('mask');\\n      const maskIdx = wc.pigment();\\n      console.log(`[demo] mask pigment index resolved to: ${maskIdx}`);\\n      for (const s of stamps) {\\n        const { gx, gy } = wc.toGrid(s.ccx, s.ccy);\\n        const gridR = s.maskR / wc.scale();\\n        wc.paintAt(gx, gy, gridR, maskIdx, 1.0);\\n      }\\n      wc.pigment(prevPigment);\\n\\n      // (e) Background preset. Paints the \\\"dawn\\\" wash everywhere;\\n      //     the masks above protect the card areas from being\\n      //     touched. Change 'dawn' to 'day'/'dusk'/'night' for\\n      //     other moods, or define your own in BACKGROUND_PRESETS.\\n      applyBackgroundPreset('dawn');\\n\\n      // (f) Restore normal evaporation + reset to a default\\n      //     pigment so the user's first brush stroke is rose.\\n      wc.evaporation(10);\\n      wc.pigment('rose');\\n\\n      // (g) Hover handler: trigger a small splash JUST BELOW each\\n      //     card on pointerenter. The splash has to land outside\\n      //     the masked card area or it'd be blocked \\u2014 masked cells\\n      //     reject pigment, wet, pressure, and velocity. Splashing\\n      //     below the card lands the splash in the dawn-washed\\n      //     un-masked area, creating a \\\"drip from the card\\\" effect.\\n      //\\n      //     If you want hover splashes ON the card surface instead,\\n      //     shrink the mask radius (in step d) so the splash radius\\n      //     extends beyond the mask.\\n      //\\n      //     Per-card cooldown of 600ms so rapid hovers don't pile up.\\n      for (const stamp of stamps) {\\n        const card = stamp.card;\\n        if (card._washHoverHandler) {\\n          card.removeEventListener('pointerenter', card._washHoverHandler);\\n        }\\n        card._lastSplashAt = card._lastSplashAt || 0;\\n        const handler = (e) => {\\n          const now = performance.now();\\n          if (now - card._lastSplashAt < 600) return;\\n          card._lastSplashAt = now;\\n\\n          // Re-read position (card may have moved since setup)\\n          const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\\n          const hr = host.getBoundingClientRect();\\n          const cx = rect.left + rect.width / 2 - hr.left;\\n          // Land splash 24px below card bottom \\u2014 outside the mask\\n          const cy = rect.bottom - hr.top + 24;\\n\\n          const prev = wc.pigment();\\n          wc.pigment(stamp.pigment);\\n          wc.splash(\\n            [{ x: cx, y: cy, velocity: 18 }],\\n            'default',\\n            { radius: 80, pressure: 0.6 }\\n          );\\n          wc.pigment(prev);\\n        };\\n        card.addEventListener('pointerenter', handler);\\n        card._washHoverHandler = handler;\\n      }\\n\\n      console.groupEnd();\\n    }\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // RESIZE\\n    // ============================================================\\n    let resizeTimer = null;\\n    window.addEventListener('resize', () => {\\n      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);\\n      resizeTimer = setTimeout(() => {\\n        resizeTimer = null;\\n        setup();\\n      }, 200);\\n    });\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // KICK IT OFF\\n    // ============================================================\\n    requestAnimationFrame(() => setup());\\n  </script>\\n</body>\\n</html>\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/portfolio-demo.html\"}"

No fills \u2014 only strokes (the lib\n    // traces each stroke as a pen path). 0.95, pigment: 'blue', strength: 0.07, radius: 220 },\n      ],\n      night: [\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.10, pigment: 'blue', strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'blue', strength: 0.22, radius: 320 },\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.10, pigment: 'blue', strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.95, pigment: 'blue', strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\n      ],\n    };\n\n    function applyBackgroundPreset(name) {\n      const preset = BACKGROUND_PRESETS[name];\n      if (!preset) {\n        console.warn(`[demo] background preset '${name}' not found`);\n        return;\n      }\n      const w = host.clientWidth, h = host.clientHeight;\n      for (const p of preset) {\n        const pigIdx = PIGMENT_INDICES[p.pigment];\n        if (pigIdx === undefined) continue;\n        const px = p.x * w;\n        const py = p.y * h;\n        const { gx, gy } = wc.toGrid(px, py);\n        const gridR = p.radius / wc.scale();\n        wc.paintAt(gx, gy, gridR, pigIdx, p.strength);\n      }\n    }\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // CANVAS CREATE\n    // ============================================================\n    const host = document.getElementById('canvas-host');\n    const wc = window.Washes.create(host, {\n      qualityHint: 'auto-mobile',\n      cursorPreview: false,\n    });\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // SETUP \u2014 runs once on first paint, re-runs on resize.\n    // Idempotent. Order matches the user-spec'd flow:\n    //   (a) dry the canvas\n    //   (b) read card bounds\n    //   (c) paint SVGs within those bounds\n    //   (d) mask over card bounds\n    //   (e) apply background preset\n    //   (f) restore evaporation, set default pigment\n    //   (g) re-bind hover handlers\n    // ============================================================\n    function setup() {\n      console.group('[demo] setup');\n\n      // (a) Dry the canvas \u2014 high evaporation so any setup-\n      //     painted pigment stays sharp instead of bleeding.\n      wc.evaporation(50);\n      wc.removeMask();\n      wc.background(null);  // no CSS gradient \u2014 pure canvas paint\n\n      // (b) Read card bounds. Each card is a transparent envelope;\n      //     its bounding rect = where we'll paint SVG + mask.\n      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('.card');\n      const hostRect = host.getBoundingClientRect();\n      const stamps = [];\n\n      console.log(`[demo] found ${cards.length} card(s) | scale=${wc.scale()}`);\n\n      for (const card of cards) {\n        const svgKey = card.dataset.svg;\n        const svgText = MARKS[svgKey];\n        if (!svgText) {\n          console.warn(`[demo] no SVG for data-svg=\"${svgKey}\"`);\n          continue;\n        }\n\n        const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\n\n        // (c) SVG bounds: positioned in the upper portion of the\n        //     card where .card-content text block doesn't cover.\n        //     Horizontally centered; vertical center at ~25% of\n        //     card height. Sized to fit (55% of card width, capped\n        //     at 160px).\n        const cx = rect.left - hostRect.left + rect.width / 2;\n        const cy = rect.top - hostRect.top + rect.height * 0.25;\n        const size = Math.min(160, rect.width * 0.55);\n        const half = size / 2;\n\n        const tl = wc.toGrid(cx - half, cy - half);\n        const br = wc.toGrid(cx + half, cy + half);\n        const bounds = {\n          x: tl.gx,\n          y: tl.gy,\n          w: br.gx - tl.gx,\n          h: br.gy - tl.gy,\n        };\n\n        const points = wc.traceSVG(svgText, {\n          animate: false,\n          bounds: bounds,\n          brushSize: 26,\n          strength: 0.95,\n        });\n\n        console.log(\n          `[demo] ${svgKey}: rect=${rect.left.toFixed(0)},${rect.top.toFixed(0)} ` +\n          `${rect.width.toFixed(0)}\u00d7${rect.height.toFixed(0)} | ` +\n          `svg center=(${cx.toFixed(0)},${cy.toFixed(0)}) size=${size.toFixed(0)} | ` +\n          `bounds(grid)=${bounds.x.toFixed(1)},${bounds.y.toFixed(1)} ` +\n          `${bounds.w.toFixed(1)}\u00d7${bounds.h.toFixed(1)} | ` +\n          `traceSVG returned ${points} point(s)`\n        );\n\n        // For masking: center of card + half-diagonal radius\n        // (circumscribed circle that covers the rectangular bounds).\n        const ccx = rect.left - hostRect.left + rect.width / 2;\n        const ccy = rect.top - hostRect.top + rect.height / 2;\n        const maskR = Math.sqrt(\n          rect.width * rect.width + rect.height * rect.height\n        ) / 2 * 1.05;\n\n        stamps.push({\n          card: card,\n          ccx: ccx,\n          ccy: ccy,\n          maskR: maskR,\n          pigment: PIGMENT_BY_CARD[svgKey] || 'rose',\n        });\n      }\n\n      // (d) Mask over card bounds. Mask pigment freezes cells;\n      //     all subsequent paint ops (the background preset wash\n      //     below, AND any pointer drawing) skip masked cells.\n      //\n      //     paintAt's mask branch checks `pigmentIdx === MASK_INDEX`\n      //     (numeric -4). Passing the string 'mask' silently fails.\n      //     We round-trip 'mask' \u2192 setter \u2192 getter to get the\n      //     numeric index without hardcoding it.\n      const prevPigment = wc.pigment();\n      wc.pigment('mask');\n      const maskIdx = wc.pigment();\n      console.log(`[demo] mask pigment index resolved to: ${maskIdx}`);\n      for (const s of stamps) {\n        const { gx, gy } = wc.toGrid(s.ccx, s.ccy);\n        const gridR = s.maskR / wc.scale();\n        wc.paintAt(gx, gy, gridR, maskIdx, 1.0);\n      }\n      wc.pigment(prevPigment);\n\n      // (e) Background preset. Paints the \"dawn\" wash everywhere;\n      //     masks above protect the card areas. To switch moods,\n      //     change 'dawn' to 'day' / 'dusk' / 'night', or define\n      //     your own in BACKGROUND_PRESETS above.\n      applyBackgroundPreset('dawn');\n\n      // (f) Restore normal evaporation. The setup painted stamps\n      //     will continue drying naturally over the next ~2 sec.\n      //     Active pigment back to rose for the user's first\n      //     brush stroke.\n      wc.evaporation(10);\n      wc.pigment('rose');\n\n      // (g) Hover handler. Mask = full card bounds means any\n      //     splash AT card center would be blocked (masked cells\n      //     reject pigment, wet, pressure). So we land the splash\n      //     just BELOW each card \u2014 outside the mask, in the dawn-\n      //     washed area \u2014 for a \"drip from card\" effect.\n      //\n      //     Per-card 600ms cooldown so rapid hovers don't pile up.\n      //     Old listener removed before adding the new one (setup\n      //     runs again on resize, so we can't accumulate).\n      for (const stamp of stamps) {\n        const card = stamp.card;\n        if (card._washHoverHandler) {\n          card.removeEventListener('pointerenter', card._washHoverHandler);\n        }\n        card._lastSplashAt = card._lastSplashAt || 0;\n        const handler = (e) => {\n          const now = performance.now();\n          if (now - card._lastSplashAt < 600) return;\n          card._lastSplashAt = now;\n\n          const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\n          const hr = host.getBoundingClientRect();\n          const cx = rect.left + rect.width / 2 - hr.left;\n          const cy = rect.bottom - hr.top + 24;  // 24px below card\n\n          const prev = wc.pigment();\n          wc.pigment(stamp.pigment);\n          wc.splash(\n            [{ x: cx, y: cy, velocity: 18 }],\n            'default',\n            { radius: 80, pressure: 0.6 }\n          );\n          wc.pigment(prev);\n        };\n        card.addEventListener('pointerenter', handler);\n        card._washHoverHandler = handler;\n      }\n\n      console.groupEnd();\n    }\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // RESIZE\n    // ============================================================\n    let resizeTimer = null;\n    window.addEventListener('resize', () => {\n      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);\n      resizeTimer = setTimeout(() => {\n        resizeTimer = null;\n        setup();\n      }, 200);\n    });\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // KICK IT OFF\n    // ============================================================\n    requestAnimationFrame(() => setup());\n  </script>\n</body>\n</html>\n",

(one per card)\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // Each viewBox is 200\\u00d7200. No fills \\u2014 only strokes (the lib\\n    // traces each stroke as a pen path). Stroke colors use the\\n    // lib's chroma-key pigment hexes:\\n    //   #FF0000 \\u2192 rose pigment\\n    //   #FFFF00 \\u2192 yellow pigment\\n    //   #0000FF \\u2192 blue pigment\\n    const MARKS = {\\n      'mark-1': `<svg viewBox=\\\"0 0 200 200\\\" xmlns=\\\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\\\">\\n        <path d=\\\"M 30 60 C 50 110, 100 130, 170 100\\\"\\n              fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"#FF0000\\\" stroke-width=\\\"3\\\" />\\n        <circle cx=\\\"160\\\" cy=\\\"60\\\" r=\\\"6\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"#FF0000\\\" stroke-width=\\\"3\\\" />\\n      </svg>`,\\n\\n      'mark-2': `<svg viewBox=\\\"0 0 200 200\\\" xmlns=\\\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\\\">\\n        <path d=\\\"M 100 100 C 100 60, 140 60, 140 100 C 140 150, 80 150, 80 100 C 80 40, 160 40, 160 110\\\"\\n              fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"#FFFF00\\\" stroke-width=\\\"3\\\" />\\n      </svg>`,\\n\\n      'mark-3': `<svg viewBox=\\\"0 0 200 200\\\" xmlns=\\\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\\\">\\n        <path d=\\\"M 40 50 C 80 80, 120 120, 160 150\\\"\\n              fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"#0000FF\\\" stroke-width=\\\"3\\\" />\\n        <path d=\\\"M 160 50 C 120 80, 80 120, 40 150\\\"\\n              fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"#0000FF\\\" stroke-width=\\\"3\\\" />\\n      </svg>`,\\n    };\\n\\n    const PIGMENT_BY_CARD = {\\n      'mark-1': 'rose',\\n      'mark-2': 'yellow',\\n      'mark-3': 'blue',\\n    };\\n    const PIGMENT_INDICES = { rose: 0, yellow: 1, blue: 2 };\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // BACKGROUND PRESETS\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // Each preset is an array of paint operations applied via\\n    // paintAt. Position is fractional (0..1) of canvas dimensions;\\n    // strength is per-stamp 0..1; radius is in display pixels.\\n    // The mask laid over card bounds protects those areas from\\n    // being touched.\\n    const BACKGROUND_PRESETS = {\\n      dawn: [\\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.10, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.10, radius: 220 },\\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.12, radius: 260 },\\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.15, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.10, radius: 220 },\\n        { x: 0.05, y: 0.50, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.06, radius: 180 },\\n        { x: 0.95, y: 0.50, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.06, radius: 180 },\\n        { x: 0.15, y: 0.95, pigment: 'rose',   strength: 0.08, radius: 200 },\\n        { x: 0.85, y: 0.95, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.10, radius: 230 },\\n      ],\\n      day: [\\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.20, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.10, radius: 220 },\\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.10, radius: 260 },\\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.20, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.10, radius: 220 },\\n        { x: 0.20, y: 0.90, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.08, radius: 200 },\\n        { x: 0.80, y: 0.90, pigment: 'yellow', strength: 0.08, radius: 200 },\\n      ],\\n      dusk: [\\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.10, pigment: 'rose', strength: 0.14, radius: 250 },\\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'rose', strength: 0.16, radius: 290 },\\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.10, pigment: 'rose', strength: 0.14, radius: 250 },\\n        { x: 0.20, y: 0.95, pigment: 'blue', strength: 0.07, radius: 220 },\\n        { x: 0.80, y: 0.95, pigment: 'blue', strength: 0.07, radius: 220 },\\n      ],\\n      night: [\\n        { x: 0.10, y: 0.10, pigment: 'blue', strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.05, pigment: 'blue', strength: 0.22, radius: 320 },\\n        { x: 0.90, y: 0.10, pigment: 'blue', strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\\n        { x: 0.50, y: 0.95, pigment: 'blue', strength: 0.18, radius: 280 },\\n      ],\\n    };\\n\\n    function applyBackgroundPreset(name) {\\n      const preset = BACKGROUND_PRESETS[name];\\n      if (!preset) {\\n        console.warn(`[demo] background preset '${name}' not found`);\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      const w = host.clientWidth, h = host.clientHeight;\\n      for (const p of preset) {\\n        const pigIdx = PIGMENT_INDICES[p.pigment];\\n        if (pigIdx === undefined) continue;\\n        const px = p.x * w;\\n        const py = p.y * h;\\n        const { gx, gy } = wc.toGrid(px, py);\\n        const gridR = p.radius / wc.scale();\\n        wc.paintAt(gx, gy, gridR, pigIdx, p.strength);\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // CANVAS CREATE\\n    // ============================================================\\n    const host = document.getElementById('canvas-host');\\n    const wc = window.Washes.create(host, {\\n      qualityHint: 'auto-mobile',\\n      cursorPreview: false,\\n    });\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // SETUP \\u2014 runs once on first paint, re-runs on resize.\\n    // Idempotent. Order matches the user-spec'd flow:\\n    //   (a) dry the canvas\\n    //   (b) read card bounds\\n    //   (c) paint SVGs within those bounds\\n    //   (d) mask over card bounds\\n    //   (e) apply background preset\\n    //   (f) restore evaporation, set default pigment\\n    //   (g) re-bind hover handlers\\n    // ============================================================\\n    function setup() {\\n      console.group('[demo] setup');\\n\\n      // (a) Dry the canvas \\u2014 high evaporation so any setup-\\n      //     painted pigment stays sharp instead of bleeding.\\n      wc.evaporation(50);\\n      wc.removeMask();\\n      wc.background(null);  // no CSS gradient \\u2014 pure canvas paint\\n\\n      // (b) Read card bounds. Each card is a transparent envelope;\\n      //     its bounding rect = where we'll paint SVG + mask.\\n      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('.card');\\n      const hostRect = host.getBoundingClientRect();\\n      const stamps = [];\\n\\n      console.log(`[demo] found ${cards.length} card(s) | scale=${wc.scale()}`);\\n\\n      for (const card of cards) {\\n        const svgKey = card.dataset.svg;\\n        const svgText = MARKS[svgKey];\\n        if (!svgText) {\\n          console.warn(`[demo] no SVG for data-svg=\\\"${svgKey}\\\"`);\\n          continue;\\n        }\\n\\n        const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\\n\\n        // (c) SVG bounds: positioned in the upper portion of the\\n        //     card where .card-content text block doesn't cover.\\n        //     Horizontally centered; vertical center at ~25% of\\n        //     card height. Sized to fit (55% of card width, capped\\n        //     at 160px).\\n        const cx = rect.left - hostRect.left + rect.width / 2;\\n        const cy = rect.top - hostRect.top + rect.height * 0.25;\\n        const size = Math.min(160, rect.width * 0.55);\\n        const half = size / 2;\\n\\n        const tl = wc.toGrid(cx - half, cy - half);\\n        const br = wc.toGrid(cx + half, cy + half);\\n        const bounds = {\\n          x: tl.gx,\\n          y: tl.gy,\\n          w: br.gx - tl.gx,\\n          h: br.gy - tl.gy,\\n        };\\n\\n        const points = wc.traceSVG(svgText, {\\n          animate: false,\\n          bounds: bounds,\\n          brushSize: 26,\\n          strength: 0.95,\\n        });\\n\\n        console.log(\\n          `[demo] ${svgKey}: rect=${rect.left.toFixed(0)},${rect.top.toFixed(0)} ` +\\n          `${rect.width.toFixed(0)}\\u00d7${rect.height.toFixed(0)} | ` +\\n          `svg center=(${cx.toFixed(0)},${cy.toFixed(0)}) size=${size.toFixed(0)} | ` +\\n          `bounds(grid)=${bounds.x.toFixed(1)},${bounds.y.toFixed(1)} ` +\\n          `${bounds.w.toFixed(1)}\\u00d7${bounds.h.toFixed(1)} | ` +\\n          `traceSVG returned ${points} point(s)`\\n        );\\n\\n        // For masking: center of card + half-diagonal radius\\n        // (circumscribed circle that covers the rectangular bounds).\\n        const ccx = rect.left - hostRect.left + rect.width / 2;\\n        const ccy = rect.top - hostRect.top + rect.height / 2;\\n        const maskR = Math.sqrt(\\n          rect.width * rect.width + rect.height * rect.height\\n        ) / 2 * 1.05;\\n\\n        stamps.push({\\n          card: card,\\n          ccx: ccx,\\n          ccy: ccy,\\n          maskR: maskR,\\n          pigment: PIGMENT_BY_CARD[svgKey] || 'rose',\\n        });\\n      }\\n\\n      // (d) Mask over card bounds. Mask pigment freezes cells;\\n      //     all subsequent paint ops (the background preset wash\\n      //     below, AND any pointer drawing) skip masked cells.\\n      //\\n      //     paintAt's mask branch checks `pigmentIdx === MASK_INDEX`\\n      //     (numeric -4). Passing the string 'mask' silently fails.\\n      //     We round-trip 'mask' \\u2192 setter \\u2192 getter to get the\\n      //     numeric index without hardcoding it.\\n      const prevPigment = wc.pigment();\\n      wc.pigment('mask');\\n      const maskIdx = wc.pigment();\\n      console.log(`[demo] mask pigment index resolved to: ${maskIdx}`);\\n      for (const s of stamps) {\\n        const { gx, gy } = wc.toGrid(s.ccx, s.ccy);\\n        const gridR = s.maskR / wc.scale();\\n        wc.paintAt(gx, gy, gridR, maskIdx, 1.0);\\n      }\\n      wc.pigment(prevPigment);\\n\\n      // (e) Background preset. Paints the \\\"dawn\\\" wash everywhere;\\n      //     masks above protect the card areas. To switch moods,\\n      //     change 'dawn' to 'day' / 'dusk' / 'night', or define\\n      //     your own in BACKGROUND_PRESETS above.\\n      applyBackgroundPreset('dawn');\\n\\n      // (f) Restore normal evaporation. The setup painted stamps\\n      //     will continue drying naturally over the next ~2 sec.\\n      //     Active pigment back to rose for the user's first\\n      //     brush stroke.\\n      wc.evaporation(10);\\n      wc.pigment('rose');\\n\\n      // (g) Hover handler. Mask = full card bounds means any\\n      //     splash AT card center would be blocked (masked cells\\n      //     reject pigment, wet, pressure). So we land the splash\\n      //     just BELOW each card \\u2014 outside the mask, in the dawn-\\n      //     washed area \\u2014 for a \\\"drip from card\\\" effect.\\n      //\\n      //     Per-card 600ms cooldown so rapid hovers don't pile up.\\n      //     Old listener removed before adding the new one (setup\\n      //     runs again on resize, so we can't accumulate).\\n      for (const stamp of stamps) {\\n        const card = stamp.card;\\n        if (card._washHoverHandler) {\\n          card.removeEventListener('pointerenter', card._washHoverHandler);\\n        }\\n        card._lastSplashAt = card._lastSplashAt || 0;\\n        const handler = (e) => {\\n          const now = performance.now();\\n          if (now - card._lastSplashAt < 600) return;\\n          card._lastSplashAt = now;\\n\\n          const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\\n          const hr = host.getBoundingClientRect();\\n          const cx = rect.left + rect.width / 2 - hr.left;\\n          const cy = rect.bottom - hr.top + 24;  // 24px below card\\n\\n          const prev = wc.pigment();\\n          wc.pigment(stamp.pigment);\\n          wc.splash(\\n            [{ x: cx, y: cy, velocity: 18 }],\\n            'default',\\n            { radius: 80, pressure: 0.6 }\\n          );\\n          wc.pigment(prev);\\n        };\\n        card.addEventListener('pointerenter', handler);\\n        card._washHoverHandler = handler;\\n      }\\n\\n      console.groupEnd();\\n    }\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // RESIZE\\n    // ============================================================\\n    let resizeTimer = null;\\n    window.addEventListener('resize', () => {\\n      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);\\n      resizeTimer = setTimeout(() => {\\n        resizeTimer = null;\\n        setup();\\n      }, 200);\\n    });\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // KICK IT OFF\\n    // ============================================================\\n    requestAnimationFrame(() => setup());\\n  </script>\\n</body>\\n</html>\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/portfolio-demo.html\"}"

    "type": "text",
    "text": "Shipped a rewrite. Here's what's different and why.\n\n## Following your spec exactly\n\nThe flow is now:\n1. **DOM/layout ready** \u2014 `requestAnimationFrame` ensures cards have laid out before reading their rects\n2. **SVGs traced within card bounds** \u2014 each SVG is positioned in the upper portion of its card (where the text block doesn't cover), sized at 55% of card width\n3. **Mask covers each card's bounds** \u2014 circumscribed circle (radius = half-diagonal \u00d7 1.05) ensures the rectangular card area is fully protected\n4. **Background preset \"dawn\" paints over canvas** \u2014 7 paint operations across the viewport, masks protect the card areas so the wash flows around them\n\n## Structural change: nested card markup\n\nI split each card into outer `.card` (transparent envelope) + inner `.card-content` (opaque text block). The outer envelope defines bounds; the inner block holds readable text. This is what makes the SVGs actually visible \u2014 they paint in the card's full bounds, and the transparent upper portion of each card lets them show through.\n\nPrevious version had the card itself being 55% opaque cream with backdrop-blur. That was washing out the SVGs even when the lib was correctly painting them.\n\n## Hover splash moved below cards\n\nReading the lib carefully: `paintAt` checks `if (maskActive && mask[i] > MASK_THRESHOLD) continue` in EVERY paint branch \u2014 pigment, water, lift, paper, rainbow. **Masked cells reject all paint operations**. Since your spec puts a full-card mask, any splash centered ON the card would be entirely blocked.\n\nI moved the hover splash to land 24px below each card's bottom edge \u2014 outside the masked area, in the un-masked dawn-washed region. Visually it reads as a \"drip from card\" effect. If you'd rather have splashes ON the card surface, we'd need to shrink the mask so the splash radius extends past it.\n\n## Debug logging\n\nI added `console.log` calls inside setup() to make the SVG situation diagnosable. Open DevTools console and you should see something like:\n\n```\n[demo] setup\n[demo] found 3 card(s) | scale=2.5\n[demo] mark-1: rect=86,260 320\u00d7380 | svg center=(246,355) size=160 | bounds(grid)=66.4,110.0 64.0\u00d764.0 | traceSVG returned 47 point(s)\n[demo] mark-2: ...\n[demo] mark-3: ...\n[demo] mask pigment index resolved to: -4\n```\n\nThe key number is `traceSVG returned N point(s)`. If `N` is 0, the SVG parsing is failing (likely a DOMParser issue or the SVG markup isn't being recognized). If `N` > 0 but you still don't see marks, the issue is something downstream (bounds wrong, render path skipping, etc.). Either way, the console output gives us evidence.\n\n## What to verify\n\n1. **Open DevTools console** \u2014 check for `[demo] setup` group, confirm `traceSVG returned N` is non-zero for each mark.\n2. **Look at the upper portion of each card** \u2014 should see colored marks visible through the transparent area above the text block.\n3. **Hover any card** \u2014 should see a small splash appear just below the card.\n4. **Background should be a soft warm \"dawn\" wash** around the cards (rose + yellow), with the card areas remaining mostly cream/clear (protected by mask).\n\nIf you still don't see SVGs, please share the console output \u2014 I'll know exactly where it's failing.",

    "text": "Shipped three things together: the fixed demo, washes v0.94 playground, and v0.94 package.\n\n## The bug and what it actually broke\n\n`wc.toGrid(displayX, displayY)` returned `{ x, y }` in the runtime lib, but the TypeScript declaration AND the playground documentation example both said `{ gx, gy }`. I wrote my own docs that disagreed with my own implementation, then trusted the docs when writing the demo.\n\nThe downstream impact was catastrophic and silent:\n- SVG bounds: `{ x: undefined, y: undefined, w: NaN, h: NaN }` \u2014 `traceSVG` got nonsense, painted nothing visible\n- Mask paintAt: `gx=undefined, gy=undefined` \u2014 NaN math, no mask deposited\n- Background preset paintAt: same problem \u2014 no wash deposited\n\nThree iterations of \"the SVGs aren't showing up\" \u2014 every plausible theory I had (card opacity, mask blocking, wash overwhelming, evaporation too fast) was downstream of this. None of the canvas painting in `setup()` was ever landing on real cells because the coordinates were always `NaN`. The diagnostic logging I added in the last iteration is what finally surfaced it.\n\n## The fix\n\nI picked the option that aligns the most pieces: change the lib to return `{ gx, gy }`, matching:\n- Its own internal `_clientToGrid` function (line 6776) which already returns `{ gx, gy }`\n- The `paintAt(gx, gy, ...)` parameter names\n- The .d.ts type declaration I'd already written\n- The playground docs example I'd already written\n\nThe demo file didn't need to change \u2014 it was already using `.gx` / `.gy` (which is why it was broken against the old lib but works against the new one).\n\n## Honest reflection\n\nThis bug was 100% my fault and it cost the user multiple iterations of confusion. A few lessons I'd want to internalize:\n\n1. **Don't trust documentation you wrote yourself in a different turn**. When consuming an API I just wrote, I should verify by reading the implementation, not the .d.ts.\n\n2. **Silent failures from NaN math are deadly**. The lib's `paintAt` accepted `(NaN, NaN, ...)` and quietly did nothing instead of throwing or warning. I could add NaN-validation guards in the lib's public API surface. Not doing it now (out of scope) but it's the kind of defensive-design choice that catches exactly this class of bug.\n\n3. **Adding `.toFixed(N)` in diagnostic logging was the unintentional hero**. It's the only thing that caused a visible error rather than silent NaN propagation. Diagnostic code that fails loudly is more valuable than diagnostic code that fails quietly.\n\n## What you should see now\n\nOpen the demo. In DevTools console:\n\n```\n[demo] setup --- start\n[demo] found 3 card(s) | scale=1.75\n[demo] mark-1: rect=86,260 320\u00d7380 | svg center=(246,355) size=160 | bounds(grid)=37.8,140.0 91.4\u00d791.4 | traceSVG returned 47 point(s)\n[demo] mark-2: ...\n[demo] mark-3: ...\n[demo] mask pigment index resolved to: -4\n[demo] setup --- done\n```\n\nThe `bounds(grid)` numbers should now be real (e.g., `37.8,140.0 91.4\u00d791.4`) rather than `NaN,NaN NaN\u00d7NaN`. The `traceSVG returned N` should be > 0 (real number of paint points).\n\nAnd visually:\n- Three SVG marks visible in the upper portion of each card\n- Soft \"dawn\" wash (rose + yellow) around the cards\n- Card areas remain mostly clean cream (protected by mask)\n- Hover any card \u2192 small splash 24px below it\n\nIf any of this isn't right, the console output will tell us specifically where it's failing.",
    "citations": []
  }
]

    "type": "text",
    "text": " Good question \u2014 and there's a non-obvious gotcha I want to surface before coding.\n\n## The catch with `roundRect`\n\n`CanvasRenderingContext2D.roundRect()` is a 2D-canvas method. It would draw to a 2D canvas context. But the watercolor lib's mask is **not** a 2D canvas \u2014 it's a `Float32Array` indexed by grid cells (one float per simulation cell, where >0.5 means \"masked\"). The lib's `paintAt(gx, gy, gridRadius, MASK_INDEX, strength)` is the only public path to set mask values.\n\nSo a literal \"use `roundRect()`\" doesn't work \u2014 the lib doesn't have a \"stamp a 2D shape into the mask field\" API. Two options to make rounded-rectangle masks possible:\n\n### Option A \u2014 Build it from circles (no lib change)\n\nA rounded rectangle is equivalent to: a central rectangle + 4 corner circles + the gaps between. Approximated by overlapping circle stamps via `paintAt(gx, gy, radius, MASK_INDEX, 1.0)`. Specifically:\n\n- Top edge: stamp small circles along the line from `(x+r, y)` to `(x+w-r, y)` at intervals of ~`circle_radius * 0.4`\n- Same for bottom, left, right edges\n- Corners: stamp a single circle at each corner center (at distance `r` from corner)\n- Fill interior: stamp larger circles along a grid inside the rect\n\nThis works with the current lib. Costs many `paintAt` calls (probably 50-200 per rectangle), each does O(radius\u00b2) work. For 3 cards at typical card sizes, that's ~600 small paintAt calls in setup() \u2014 should be milliseconds.\n\nThe edge will be slightly bumpy (each circle's perimeter creates a small ripple in the mask boundary). With overlap of 60%+ between stamps, this is invisible at the lib's grid resolution. Fine in practice.\n\n### Option B \u2014 Add a `maskRect(x, y, w, h, radii)` API to the lib\n\nA new API that directly writes to the `mask` Float32Array using a vectorized loop with `roundRect`-style geometry. Crisper edges (single pass, exact pixel coverage), faster (one loop instead of hundreds of paintAt calls), and the API matches what you actually asked for.\n\nReal cost: implementing rounded-rectangle rasterization correctly. Not hard but takes care \u2014 for each cell in the rect, compute distance to nearest corner; if cell is inside corner radius arc OR inside the inner rect, set mask. Maybe 30 lines.\n\n### Recommendation\n\n**Build option B as a new lib API.** Reasons:\n1. You asked for `roundRect`-style crisp edges \u2014 option A is a pile of overlapping circles which is exactly NOT crisp\n2. Demos like this are likely use case (cards, panels, UI regions over watercolor)\n3. The API generalizes \u2014 anyone using the lib for portfolio sites / dashboards will want this\n4. It's the kind of small, well-bounded primitive that fits the lib's design\n\nI'd build:\n```js\nwc.maskRect(displayX, displayY, displayW, displayH, radii)\n```\n\nWhere `radii` matches the `roundRect()` API spec: a number (uniform), or an array `[topLeft, topRight, bottomRight, bottomLeft]`, or `[top, right, bottom, left]`, etc. Display-pixel coords (not grid) \u2014 most consumers think in display pixels and the lib does the conversion. Composes with existing `removeMask()` so demos can call `removeMask()` then `maskRect()` per card without ceremony.\n\nLet me build option B.",

Strength scales how\\n        // quickly a single stamp builds \\u2014 at strength=0.5, two dabs at\\n        // the brush center push above threshold.\\n        const m = mask[i] + falloff * strength;\\n        mask[i] = m > 1 ? 1 : m;\\n        if (mask[i] > MASK_THRESHOLD) {\\n          anyAboveThreshold = true;\\n          if (px < rMinX) rMinX = px;\\n          if (px > rMaxX) rMaxX = px;\\n          if (py < rMinY) rMinY = py;\\n          if (py > rMaxY) rMaxY = py;\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n    if (anyAboveThreshold) {\\n      maskActive = true;\\n      // Expand the global mask rect to include the freshly-masked cells.\\n      if (rMinX < maskRectMinX) maskRectMinX = rMinX;\\n      if (rMaxX > maskRectMaxX) maskRectMaxX = rMaxX;\\n      if (rMinY < maskRectMinY) maskRectMinY = rMinY;\\n      if (rMaxY > maskRectMaxY) maskRectMaxY = rMaxY;\\n    }\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  // Water brush: no pigment added. Adds water + pressure (drives bleed)\\n  // and lifts a fraction of deposited pigment back into suspension at\\n  // each touched cell \\u2014 same desorption shortcut the global re-wet\\n  // button uses, but localized. This lets a clean wet brush re-mobilize\\n  // dry pigment for blending, softening edges, or washing out.\\n  if (pigmentIdx === WATER_INDEX) {\\n    // v0.22 \\u2014 water-load multiplier.       "new_str": "function removeMask() {\n  if (!maskActive) return;\n  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) mask[i] = 0;\n  maskActive = false;\n  clearMaskRect();\n  // Force-full re-render: ex-masked cells reveal whatever was frozen\n  // underneath, which the active-rect-localized render wouldn't catch\n  // if those cells sit outside the rect.\n  render(true);\n}\n\n// v0.95 \u2014 Mask a rounded rectangle directly into the mask field.\n//\n// Args:\n//   displayX, displayY, displayW, displayH \u2014 rectangle in display\n//     pixels (top-left + size). Same coord space as\n//     getBoundingClientRect, getElementById('host').clientWidth, etc.\n//   radii \u2014 corner radius/radii, in display pixels. Matches the\n//     CanvasRenderingContext2D.roundRect() spec:\n//       \u2022 number  \u2192 uniform radius all four corners\n//       \u2022 [a]     \u2192 uniform radius all four corners\n//       \u2022 [a, b]  \u2192 [top-left+bottom-right, top-right+bottom-left]\n//       \u2022 [a, b, c] \u2192 [top-left, top-right+bottom-left, bottom-right]\n//       \u2022 [a, b, c, d] \u2192 [top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left]\n//\n// Compared to a circle of paintAt(MASK_INDEX) stamps:\n//   \u2022 Crisper edges \u2014 single rasterization pass, not many overlapping\n//     circle perimeters.\n//   \u2022 Faster \u2014 one O(w\u00b7h) loop vs many paintAt calls.\n//   \u2022 Shape matches what you actually want (cards, panels, UI regions).\n//\n// Mask cells are set to 1.0 (fully frozen). For partial / soft-edged\n// masks use paintAt(MASK_INDEX) with strength < 1.\nfunction maskRectDisplay(displayX, displayY, displayW, displayH, radii) {\n  if (!isFinite(displayX) || !isFinite(displayY) ||\n      !isFinite(displayW) || !isFinite(displayH) ||\n      displayW <= 0 || displayH <= 0) {\n    console.warn('maskRect: invalid bounds', { displayX, displayY, displayW, displayH });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Normalize radii to [topLeft, topRight, bottomRight, bottomLeft].\n  // Matches the CSS / roundRect() convention.\n  let r0, r1, r2, r3;\n  if (radii === undefined || radii === null) {\n    r0 = r1 = r2 = r3 = 0;\n  } else if (typeof radii === 'number') {\n    r0 = r1 = r2 = r3 = +radii;\n  } else if (Array.isArray(radii)) {\n    if (radii.length === 1)      { r0 = r1 = r2 = r3 = +radii[0]; }\n    else if (radii.length === 2) { r0 = r2 = +radii[0]; r1 = r3 = +radii[1]; }\n    else if (radii.length === 3) { r0 = +radii[0]; r1 = r3 = +radii[1]; r2 = +radii[2]; }\n    else                          { r0 = +radii[0]; r1 = +radii[1]; r2 = +radii[2]; r3 = +radii[3]; }\n  } else {\n    console.warn('maskRect: unrecognized radii shape, treating as 0', radii);\n    r0 = r1 = r2 = r3 = 0;\n  }\n  // Sanitize: NaN \u2192 0, negative \u2192 0\n  if (!isFinite(r0) || r0 < 0) r0 = 0;\n  if (!isFinite(r1) || r1 < 0) r1 = 0;\n  if (!isFinite(r2) || r2 < 0) r2 = 0;\n  if (!isFinite(r3) || r3 < 0) r3 = 0;\n  // Clamp each radius to half the smaller side (CSS does this too)\n  const maxR = Math.min(displayW, displayH) / 2;\n  if (r0 > maxR) r0 = maxR;\n  if (r1 > maxR) r1 = maxR;\n  if (r2 > maxR) r2 = maxR;\n  if (r3 > maxR) r3 = maxR;\n\n  // Convert display\u2192grid via the same math as toGrid().\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const gxScale = GW / rect.width;\n  const gyScale = GH / rect.height;\n  const gxOf = (dx) => (dx - rect.left) * gxScale;\n  const gyOf = (dy) => (dy - rect.top)  * gyScale;\n  const gScaleAvg = (gxScale + gyScale) * 0.5;  // radii are uniform pixels\n\n  // Grid-coordinate rectangle bounds + radii. Grid radii are display\n  // radii \u00d7 average grid-per-display ratio (close enough to isotropic\n  // for typical aspect ratios; canvas display is square-pixel).\n  const gx0 = gxOf(displayX);\n  const gy0 = gyOf(displayY);\n  const gx1 = gxOf(displayX + displayW);\n  const gy1 = gyOf(displayY + displayH);\n  const gr0 = r0 * gScaleAvg;\n  const gr1 = r1 * gScaleAvg;\n  const gr2 = r2 * gScaleAvg;\n  const gr3 = r3 * gScaleAvg;\n\n  // Pixel-bounds of the iteration. Clip to grid range.\n  const iMinX = Math.max(0, Math.floor(gx0));\n  const iMaxX = Math.min(GW - 1, Math.ceil(gx1));\n  const iMinY = Math.max(0, Math.floor(gy0));\n  const iMaxY = Math.min(GH - 1, Math.ceil(gy1));\n  if (iMaxX < iMinX || iMaxY < iMinY) return;\n\n  // Corner-center coords (where the corner-arc origin sits)\n  // tl: (gx0 + r0,  gy0 + r0)\n  // tr: (gx1 - r1,  gy0 + r1)\n  // br: (gx1 - r2,  gy1 - r2)\n  // bl: (gx0 + r3,  gy1 - r3)\n  const tlcx = gx0 + gr0, tlcy = gy0 + gr0, tlr2 = gr0 * gr0;\n  const trcx = gx1 - gr1, trcy = gy0 + gr1, trr2 = gr1 * gr1;\n  const brcx = gx1 - gr2, brcy = gy1 - gr2, brr2 = gr2 * gr2;\n  const blcx = gx0 + gr3, blcy = gy1 - gr3, blr2 = gr3 * gr3;\n\n  // Track which cells crossed the threshold for maskRect bookkeeping\n  let anyAbove = false;\n  let rMinX = GW, rMaxX = -1, rMinY = GH, rMaxY = -1;\n\n  for (let py = iMinY; py <= iMaxY; py++) {\n    for (let px = iMinX; px <= iMaxX; px++) {\n      // Inside-or-not test: check which \"zone\" the cell sits in.\n      // The non-rounded interior of the rect is anything past each\n      // corner's arc center, on the interior side. We test each\n      // corner's arc only if the cell is in that corner's quadrant.\n      let inside = false;\n      if (px < tlcx && py < tlcy) {\n        const dx = px - tlcx, dy = py - tlcy;\n        if (dx*dx + dy*dy <= tlr2) inside = true;\n      } else if (px > trcx && py < trcy) {\n        const dx = px - trcx, dy = py - trcy;\n        if (dx*dx + dy*dy <= trr2) inside = true;\n      } else if (px > brcx && py > brcy) {\n        const dx = px - brcx, dy = py - brcy;\n        if (dx*dx + dy*dy <= brr2) inside = true;\n      } else if (px < blcx && py > blcy) {\n        const dx = px - blcx, dy = py - blcy;\n        if (dx*dx + dy*dy <= blr2) inside = true;\n      } else {\n        // Cell is in one of the \"straight\" zones (between corners) \u2014\n        // straightforward rect-inside test against the outer bounds.\n        if (px >= gx0 && px <= gx1 && py >= gy0 && py <= gy1) {\n          inside = true;\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (inside) {\n        const i = py * GW + px;\n        mask[i] = 1;\n        anyAbove = true;\n        if (px < rMinX) rMinX = px;\n        if (px > rMaxX) rMaxX = px;\n        if (py < rMinY) rMinY = py;\n        if (py > rMaxY) rMaxY = py;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (anyAbove) {\n    maskActive = true;\n    if (rMinX < maskRectMinX) maskRectMinX = rMinX;\n    if (rMaxX > maskRectMaxX) maskRectMaxX = rMaxX;\n    if (rMinY < maskRectMinY) maskRectMinY = rMinY;\n    if (rMaxY > maskRectMaxY) maskRectMaxY = rMaxY;\n  }\n}\n\n",
